chunks.text: Dedicated to hosting presentations at the intersection of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, the EECS Colloquium offers a mix of short talks by faculty describing their latest research results as well as internationally distinguished speakers. It provides an opportunity for an exchange of ideas between our department’s community and the outside world. Wednesdays 4:00-5:00 PM, HP Auditorium, 306 Soda Hall (unless otherwise noted). Light refreshments served at 3:30 pm. Subscribe to the eecs-announce mailing list through the web interface or via email so you can be apprised of upcoming talks. Talk recordings will become available four business days after each session.

Jon Kleinberg presents 'Formal Models of Language Generation' on April 22, 2026 via YouTube Webinar. Eli Yablonovitch presents 'What I Wish I Had Known, as I Searched for the First 3d Photonic Bandgap' on September 12, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. David Tse presents 'Realizing Nakamoto’s Dream: One-Time Signatures, Garbled Circuits and Zero-Knowledge Proofs' on September 17, 2025 via YouTube Webinar.

Serina Chang presents 'Inferring and simulating human behaviors for societal decision-making' on September 24, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. Dina Katabi presents 'Beyond Wearables: The Rise of Invisible AI-Powered Health Tech!' on October 1, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. Krysta Svore presents 'Why Quantum, Why Now: The Case for Better Qubits and Better Data' on October 15, 2025 at 12:00 pm at Simons Institute Main Auditorium via YouTube Webinar.

Vahid Vahedi presents 'Engineering the future of semiconductors with atomic precision and AI' on October 22, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. Ali Hajimiri presents 'The Power of Working in Concert' on October 29, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. Wes Holliday presents 'Back and Forth Between Computing and Social Choice' on November 5, 2025 via YouTube Webinar.

Meredith Morris presents 'HCI for AGI' on November 12, 2025 at International House. Maneesh Agrawala presents 'Making is Decision Making' on November 19, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. Bill Dally presents 'Trends in Deep Learning Hardware' on December 3, 2025 via YouTube Webinar. Ece Kamar presents 'Navigating the AI Horizon: Promises, Perils, and the Power of Collaboration' on December 10, 2025 via YouTube Webinar.
